description: Evaluates the skill's ability to run structured opportunity discovery sessions for Legalhero PMs — extracting, scoring, organising, and persisting opportunities from raw inputs.

evals.prompt:
I just finished a 45-minute interview with Melissa, one of our partner lawyers. She's been on the platform for about 8 months and handles mostly Kündigungsschutz cases. Here are my rough notes:

- She always has 30-40 active cases at once. Her main pain is that she has no idea which cases need attention TODAY. The case list is just sorted by Frist and she spends 10-15 minutes every morning manually scanning through everything to figure out what's actually urgent.
- She mentioned that clients call her directly on her personal mobile when they don't get updates — she said this happens maybe 3-4x per week and it's really disruptive because she loses her focus.
- She uploads documents to CaseForce, but then has to also email copies to the RSV separately because apparently they don't always pull from CaseForce. She said 'I never know if they've seen it or not'.
- She tried using the AI document generation feature once, gave up because the output was wrong (wrong party name in the header) and hasn't touched it since. Said she doesn't trust it.
- Small thing: she has to manually enter her timesheet hours into a separate Excel sheet that's emailed to the finance team. Seems like something that should be in the platform.

Can you process these notes and create the opportunity file?
evals.expected_output: A structured opportunity file saved to the discovery/opportunities folder with: (1) all 5 observations turned into named opportunities with clear problem statements (not solution statements), (2) each opportunity scored with Pain × Reach × Frequency with explicit evidence, (3) a parent-child tree where appropriate (e.g., case awareness and client disruption may be children of a 'lack of case prioritisation visibility' parent), (4) cross-references to existing opportunity files in the folder, (5) strategic relevance noted for high-score opportunities, (6) gaps/validation questions identified, (7) a weekly discovery summary delivered to the user after the file is saved.

assertions.text: All 5 observations from the interview are captured as distinct opportunity entries
Every opportunity is framed as a problem/need, not a solution (no 'add a dashboard', 'fix the AI', 'build an integration' as opportunity titles)
Every opportunity has a numeric score with Pain, Reach, and Frequency all rated on the 1-3 scale with evidence provided for each dimension
An opportunity file is actually saved to the /discovery/opportunities/ folder (not just shown in chat)
The output file follows the template structure: includes Opportunity Summary table, Opportunity Tree, Detailed Opportunities with dimension tables, Cross-References, and Source Notes sections
A weekly discovery summary is delivered to the user after the file is saved, including top opportunities and recommended next focus

evals.prompt:
Weekly discovery check-in. Here's what I have from this week:

1. Analytics from PostHog: The 'Neue Nachricht' (new message) button in the case communication tab has a 34% abandonment rate — users click it, start typing, then close without sending. This has been consistent for 6 weeks.

2. Three support tickets this week all describing the same thing: lawyers are getting confused about which 'status' to set when a case is waiting for a response from the opposing party. One ticket said 'there's no status that fits — I just leave it as in Bearbeitung even though nothing is happening'.

3. Internal ops feedback: The team is spending about 2 hours per week manually re-assigning cases that were originally sent to the wrong lawyer (wrong Rechtsgebiet match). They think it happens on maybe 10-15% of new cases.

I don't have any interviews from this week. What should I do with all this?
evals.expected_output: The skill should process all three inputs without requiring an interview — analytics signals, support tickets, and internal feedback are all valid opportunity sources. It should: (1) extract distinct opportunities from each source, (2) help the user understand that the analytics abandonment signal likely indicates an underlying problem (not just a UI issue), (3) the missing status option is a clear workflow gap, (4) the 10-15% mis-match rate is a quantified problem worth escalating, (5) score all opportunities with the available evidence noting where data is limited, (6) save a structured opportunity file, (7) note that the lack of interviews this week is a gap and suggest scheduling one for next week, (8) deliver a summary.

assertions.text: All three input sources (analytics, support tickets, internal feedback) are processed and yield distinct opportunities
The analytics abandonment signal is framed as an opportunity/problem, not just reported as a metric
The mis-match rate opportunity includes the quantified evidence (10-15% of new cases, 2h/week ops time)
The skill notes the absence of user interviews this week and prompts the user to schedule one
An opportunity file is saved to the discovery/opportunities folder
Scores are calculated correctly using Pain × Reach × Frequency formula and flagged where evidence is limited

evals.prompt:
I had a call yesterday with our RSV contact at ARAG (one of our insurance partners). Quick call, not a formal discovery session, but she said a few things I want to capture before I forget:

- They want monthly reports on case status distribution — they currently get nothing systematic and have to ask us ad hoc. She said 'we're flying blind on your portfolio'.
- ARAG is under regulatory pressure to reduce average case resolution time. They asked whether we can give them predictive data on case duration based on case type and complexity. Currently no such feature exists.
- She mentioned that when lawyers submit invoices late, ARAG processes them in bulk at month-end and they can't reconcile which invoice belongs to which case. This creates accounting overhead for them.
- She said two other RSV partners have apparently asked about an API access option — she didn't know if we were building this but wanted to flag it.

Please capture these as opportunities.
evals.expected_output: The skill should handle the RSV stakeholder perspective — this is a different persona (insurer, not lawyer or client). It should: (1) frame all 4 observations as opportunities from the RSV perspective, (2) score them considering that RSV is a critical B2B customer/partner and their pain has business viability implications, (3) note that the API request is second-hand (should be verified with the other RSVs before escalating), (4) connect the reporting gap to Legalhero's Data-Driven Infrastructure pillar, (5) note that 'predictive case duration' is partially a solution idea from the user, and reframe it as the underlying need, (6) save the file, (7) note that a follow-up interview with ARAG product/ops team would strengthen these signals.

assertions.text: All 4 observations are captured as opportunities with RSV as the stakeholder perspective
The predictive data request is reframed from a solution ('predictive feature') to an underlying need ('RSV lacks visibility into expected case duration')
The secondhand API signal is flagged as needing validation before being treated as a confirmed opportunity
Strategic relevance to Legalhero pillars is noted for at least the reporting and data gaps
An opportunity file is saved to the discovery/opportunities folder with correct RSV stakeholder perspective
Source quality is rated as medium or low given this was an informal call, not a structured interview, and limitations are noted
